Under the tropical savanna (Aw) classification, Dhaka endures a stark climatic rhythm. Summers from March to June are scorching and humid, with temperatures climbing into the high 30s°C (100s°F) before the monsoon unleashes from June to October. Torrential rains routinely flood streets, and humidity rarely dips below 70%. Winters, from November to February, are mild and sunny, with pleasant daytime highs around 25°C (77°F). Packing requires light, breathable cottons for the heat, a sturdy umbrella or raincoat for the deluge, and a light sweater for cool winter evenings.

The best weather window is November through February, when the sky is clear and the air drier. Notable phenomena include the violent pre-monsoon nor’westers, known locally as Kalboishakhi, which can bring hail and destructive winds. Monsoon season regularly triggers catastrophic urban flooding, while the Bay of Bengal spawns occasional cyclones that can lash the coast and ripple into the city with heavy rain and storm surges.
Winter mornings often cloak Dhaka in a thick, lingering fog.

## Climate for Dhaka in July

The warmest day on average in Dhaka in July is 14.07 with 31°C | 87.7°F. The coldest day is 24.07 with 25.5°C | 77.9°F.
In the first third of the month (days 1–10), the average high reaches 30.6°C | 87.1°F and the average low is 25.7°C | 78.3°F. During days 11–20, highs average 30.7°C | 87.3°F and lows 25.8°C | 78.4°F. In the final third (days 21–months end), the average highs are around 30.6°C | 87.1°F and lows drop to 25.6°C | 78.1°F.
The warmest 10-day period overall is between days 11-20, with average highs of 27.8°C | 82°F. The coldest period is from days 21-31, with average lows of 27.6°C | 81.7°F.

In July, Dhaka sees daily precipitation levels ranging from around 8.66/0.34 mm/in to 13.04/0.51 mm/in.
The rainiest day of the month typically falls on the 13.07, with an average of 13.04/0.51 mm/in, while the driest day is the 24.07, with only 8.66/0.
34 mm/in.
In the first ten days of the month, the average daily rainfall is 10.40/0.41 mm/in, with 10 days exceeding 1 mm of rain.
The second third (days 11–20) sees 10.90/0.43 mm/in on average and 10 rainy days.
The final third (days 21–30/31) averages 11.10/0.44 mm/in with just 11 rainy days.
The wettest 10-day period occurs between days 21-31, with an average of 11.10/0.44 mm/in.
The driest stretch is from days 1-10, averaging only 8.66/0.34 mm/in.

The month of July in Dhaka brings humidity between 67% and 81%.
31\.07 is the most humid day (81%), while 02.07 is the driest (67%).
The early part of the month sees average humidity of 69%, the middle 76%, and the final days 78%.
Humidity is highest during 21-31, and lowest during 1-10.

part.text:
**Dhaka, Bangladesh — Current Weather**

- **Temperature**: 26°C (feels like 24.5°C)
- **Conditions**: Light drizzle, 100% cloud cover
- **Humidity**: 93% | **Wind**: 10.4 kph
